that gives me an hour and my stack of my clothes is about 30 minutes, okay, so that's kind of the time line, okay, so this is the first thing I give them, the epiphany bridge to how I'm gonna have my opportunity to switch, hopefully they're in, but now it turns out it's okay, now I'm gonna walk you guys through, and I traditionally, I'm gonna walk you three different secrets, because I love secrets, all my books are dot com secrets, expert secrets, traffic secrets, like I love secrets, so I'm gonna go three secrets, secret number one is this, secret number two, secret number three, okay, now the strategy behind this is, again, everything I'm doing, there's a domino, right, everyone that's buying something, there's like the big domino, here's a domino, right, then we gotta knock over, I heard Tim Ferris talk one time, and someone asked him, like, okay, can we follow you around all day with the camera and show people what you do, and he's like, my life is really boring, and he's like, if you were to actually see me, he's like, you'd be really confused, like I wake up in the morning, he's like, I drink some coffee, I meditate, I go for a jog, I take a nap, read a book, he's like, I might spend a week or two weeks or a month trying to figure out all the different things that I could be doing, like what's the one big domino, if I could knock that domino down, would either knock down all the other dominoes or make them irrelevant, okay, and I heard him say that, and I was like, oh my gosh, if anything is true in selling, right, what a lot of people try to do on stage, they try to sell this and this and this, and they're trying to close all these concerns, right, like if you're selling one-on-one, that's what you do, you make the main pitch, and then you're like, what's your concern, and you knock it off, knock it off, knock it off, knock it off, with stage presenting, it's not that way, my goal is to figure out like, what's the one thing that I gotta get in the bleed, if I get in the bleed of that one thing, all their other concerns disappear, right, because if I can convince you that, for you, the only possible way for you to techniques your company in the next 12 months is by doing this program, if I can convince you that, you're like, well Russell, what should I use for my email auto responder, well how much is it gonna cost for, really like all those concerns disappear, cause you're like, I don't know any answers, but the only way for me to do this, therefore I must do it, right, so I'm always trying to figure out in any sales presentation, what's the one big domino I'm gonna do, okay, if you look at this presentation, I'm not trying to knock down four dominoes, that's one of the big mistakes people make, I'm trying to figure out what's the one thing that you get them to believe, and then this is four shots at that, okay, there's different things, so my first, my origin story, again, there's this thing, I'm trying to knock down that thing, and for some people, the origin story knocks down the big domino and they're in, right, but what happens then, for a lot of people, they hear the thing and they're like, oh that's exciting, okay cool, I want a funnel, but like, and also the butt comes out, but like, is that, I run at this kind of company, is that really good for me, typically comes out, is questions they have about the vehicle? Okay, so questions about the vehicle, so I tell a story about funnels, like cool, funnels sound awesome, but I don't know if this works for my business, right, like that vehicle sounds good, but I don't know, like, do I really want a Ferrari, do I want a Ferrari, do I want my result, I don't know, and they have all these questions about the vehicle, so now, it's like this little brace that comes up here, so I'm like, crap, okay, I gotta knock down that domino, so I have to convince them, I have to, like, what's another story I can tell them that makes it so that they understand, like, that vehicle is for me, okay, so I'm gonna try and knock down that thing, and hopefully I'll knock down the big domino, okay, now after they believe, like, okay, this vehicle is for me, and then, like, the next thing comes up, is also they have all these internal fears, they're like, well, I don't know if I can actually do it, like, I don't know that, like, someone else could do it, but like, I don't know about me, and also the internal fears come up, so this is the next, the internal fears, okay, and so then my next story here is to try to destroy their internal false beliefs, so the false beliefs about the vehicle, the false beliefs about their internal ability to execute on with the vehicle, and then the third thing they transition to is then people come out with their external beliefs, who else are they gonna blame about why it can't work? Now I gotta take that, I tell a story that breaks the false beliefs about their external belief, and if I do that, now I've gotten basically three, four shots of this domino, all of the, like, the fears and the false beliefs they have have been knocked down, boom, and the domino falls over, and at that point they have to give you money, if they don't give you money, then they're lying to themselves, and it becomes this huge, like, schizophrenia for themselves, and it's really complicated, and they're like, I should have bought, I should have bought, why didn't I buy it, blah, blah, blah, blah, and all sorts of stuff, which is why the repitch I'm gonna talk about here in a minute works really, really good, because if they, if you knock down that domino through this process, and they don't lie, they are literally lying to themselves, okay, it causes all sorts of problems, so why the close rate gets so high when you do this correctly, because you broke down all these false beliefs, now they actually have the ability to go and do the thing, and the next best part, because you broke down these false beliefs, their ability to fulfill on the thing you just sold them, 10 Xs, okay, because now they don't have false beliefs holding them back, okay, all right, so the next thing here, so I gotta tell my original origin story about why I made the opportunity to switch, now I can move here, what's the vehicle? So for me, like, my vehicle I'm trying to get people in is funnels, right, so I'm like, hey, what's the story I need to tell to get them to believe that funnels are the key? Now, I've done this presentation so many times over the last four or five years, it changed all the time, but I'm trying to figure out like, how do I make this so that they believe this, okay, and again, I'm not teaching like, how do I explain this, like, number one, it's a story, in the story, I can teach them what, but I don't go into how, and then the goal of the story is to break their false belief. Only reason why people aren't giving you money and following what you do is because they don't believe you, they have some false beliefs keeping them back, okay, so I do is I go and I tell this story about whatever it is to break the false beliefs, and at the end of that, if I did my job right, they should be like, he's right, I do need a funnel, he's right, ketosis is the right kind of weight loss program for me, he's right, I do need to get a new car, he's right, like I do need the cut code knives or whatever, right, whatever your thing is, right, that's what they should believe, okay, now again, for some people, that's enough, and you could switch, transition to the stack and the close right now and get their money, okay, but again, because I'm not selling one-on-one, I don't know when the domino's knocked over for people, so that's why story's important, keep them entertained, so even if they're knocked over here, I'm still entertaining them, they're just getting more and more excited to buy it at the end, kind of telling the stories, okay, so I'm telling the stories about the what, it shows them the what, that's the teaching, teaching what, not the how, and then breaks false beliefs and then boom, okay, now what's instinctively happened to a percentage of people is like, funnels are the key, cool, ketosis is the key, cool, whatever your thing is, they're like, but I don't know if I can do it, I don't know how to build a funnel, I'm not technical, I don't know if I can actually eat healthy food all the time, I don't know if I can afford a new car, I don't know if whatever their thing is, right, they're making it their own story and their mind that's gonna keep, like this is why I can't do it, like I don't know if for most people it works, but for me it might not work, because I just know myself personally, okay, so the second big domino's, I gotta prove to them that they can actually do it, okay, so I tell a story, okay, showing them the what, breaking the false beliefs, okay, same thing's being shared, so for me it's like, click funnels, like I convince them that are funnels, and I'm not technical, I'm a tech team, I can't do it, and I'm like, cool, I'm not technical either, in fact, let me show you how I build a funnel, I show them, I'm like, this is how simple is I move things around, oh, it's so easy, and they're like, oh my gosh, I could actually do that, I believe funnels are the key, I believe I can actually do that, it's gonna be amazing, and they're like, oh, so then it's switched from the internal, so first it's the fear about the vehicle, then the fear about the internal ability to execute on the thing that you're offering them, and third thing is like, well, I believe that funnels are the way, I believe that I could actually do that, but I don't know how to get people to the funnel, so you know how to funnel them, it's gonna show up, so for me, external is the traffic, and weight loss is like, I believe ketosis is the key, I actually believe I could eat, bake it, and butter all day, that sounds really, really good, but I know when I go home, my spouse, my wife, she's gonna have candy, and the kids are there, and it's gonna be impossible, so I'm gonna blame it on the spouse, or whatever it is, so I gotta figure out what the external false belief they have, and then that's the third thing, is you got your story, we're showing them the what, and you're breaking the false beliefs, and that's the third thing, and if you do that correctly, now it's done, now it's like, I believe this is the way, I believe I can do it, there's nothing else holding me back, or else I'll have to give you money, I believe it, the domino has been knocked over, okay, and so that's the training, the teaching portion of this whole thing, you guys, you're finding their false beliefs, they're keeping them back, our whole goal is to get people the result, right, so what are the false beliefs they're keeping them from, from switching into the vehicle that you believe in, and then actually driving the vehicle up here, we identify those false beliefs on them, and from that, that's what we're creating our stories, and crafting stories around, okay, so you'll see this, again, as we show the video, you'll see me telling this, my origin story, telling this story, telling this story, telling this story, to try to break those things, that's what I'm doing, that's my entire goal, okay, is to do those things, and so those guys who bought, if you're here you bought, so therefore it worked for you, I was able to destroy it, well you believe that was the right vehicle, you believe that you can actually execute it, and you believe there's nothing external holding you back, and now you're ready to rock and roll, right, like that's what I'm looking for here, okay, all right, so that's the training, the training part of it, if your webinar's not converting, what's typically happening, a couple things, just like so you can spot check yourself, is when you go into your story, and you're going to the thing, you're gonna flip from mic, showing them what's possible mode to the teaching mode, and I just wanna restress and re-emphasize, you think you're helping them, but you're actually hindering them, because they'll get so caught up in like, oh cool, I think I can do it now, they'll go on their own way, what's gonna happen is they're gonna be like, whirr, and they'll never get to the result, okay, if you wanna serve them at the highest level, you have to focus on breaking their faults beliefs, so that they will actually come on this journey with you, okay, that is the key, I want you to understand that, okay, or anything else I wanna talk about here, make sure, okay, so that's kinda peace here, all right, I've used this Pypony Bridge script many times in here, I've used tons of kinda like bridges, I've used mini stories, you'll see, in fact, I challenge you guys as we do this, you go back and watch the actual presentation, and count how many little stories and mini stories that I tell throughout the 90 minute presentation at 10XM, you'll see a lot of them, okay, so that's the training part, this should end at about an hour, okay, now the hour we transition into us actually selling them something, so we have a chance to make them this ears this bloffer that we're so excited about sharing with them, right, and so what's interesting is that, this is where a lot of people start freaking out, now obviously a lot of salespeople here are slotty as we find them, but a lot of people like, we have this weird thing where we don't understand that selling is serving, and like, I don't know, I tried, when I first got started about this business, I got so excited, and I just wanted to give this to everybody, it's kind of funny looking back on it now, so I remember my very first event, I tried to sell an event, and I sold it for 5,000 bucks, and I think I sold three tickets, and I was like, I'm gonna be embarrassed when only three people show up, so I invited all my friends and my family, all the people that come to attend the event, so they all came, and it's not in the same room with these guys, and what's interesting is that, they're all excited and into it, but the people that were there, the three people that invested money to be there, all three of them ended up launching successful businesses, the 27 other people I invited for free, 100% of them didn't do anything ever. It's weird, there's this weird thing with commitment, when people pay, they pay attention, okay, and so for me, the more I love my audience, the more I want to charge them stuff, because those who pay, they pay attention, like, it's this weird thing, there's something weird about pulling money out of your bank account and giving it to somebody else that causes a commitment to actually be made, and so I have such a strong, it's why I have no fear of selling, because I have such strong belief, I know that in that 9,000 people in that room, we sold a little over 1,000 people, the other 8,000 people, it bothers me, because I know that they know this is true, they know they need to do this, and they're not doing anything with it, and I'm gonna see half of next year's or next year's event, and I'll be like, where are you at, and they're like, we haven't moved, do you tend to extra business, nope, okay, because they didn't make commitment, so I believe that I have a moral obligation to do everything in my power to get them to make that commitment, because as soon as they make a financial commitment, they're more likely to actually do the thing you're trying to get them to do, I want compliance, because I don't care about money, I'm not a money-motivated person, a lot of people don't believe that about me, it's true, I'm not money-motivated, I am so obsessed with the results of my customers, and I know, the biggest thing I found to get people results is charging my crap ton of money, that's it, we have an inner circle group, we just raised the price of $50,000 a year, and guess what, almost all of them are successful, it's very rare, okay, the more I charge, the more successful people are, so I, and I want to give that to you, because it's my belief pattern, and if it becomes your belief pattern, you won't feel awkward, this is the part where you're serving the most, you're forcing them to get off the line of dabbling and wondering, and making them make a commitment to actually change, okay, so with that said, I make a transition, I always try to make a little joke in the transition, and a couple things I do, number one is, typically, I can't remember in this presentation, I might have, I can't remember if I did or not, but I usually recap, in the last 60 minutes we covered a lot of stuff, I talked about this, and this, and this, and this, and how many of you guys feel a little overwhelmed, somebody showed a picture of a girl with a fire hose in her mouth, people laugh, oh, I feel a little overwhelmed, I said, that's okay, I wanted to give you guys the framework, so you had that, so when you leave here today, you've got something to start running with, but I know that there's so much more I can do for you and do with you, and I couldn't do in a 60 minute presentation, and so what I do, this is the key, I always ask permission, I learn this from, I think Joel Bauer, I'm pretty sure I learned from him, and ever since I started doing it, it changed everything, so what I normally do, as I would do my presentation, I try to sneakily slip into my pitch, and every time I do that, you get angry people who pop and I'm walking out, it got really, really awkward, and so Joel Bauer, I swear it was Joel, he told me, he's always asked for permission, and at first I was like, I'm not gonna ask him for permission to sell it, it's like the most embarrassing, scary thing ever, and one time I tried it, and what was crazy is that nobody got up, not one person got up, and I didn't get anybody telling me that that was just a pitch fest, it changed the whole perspective, so I get done, I said, I served you guys my highest level, but I know there's more, I wanna give you more, I wanna serve you more, there's so much more I wanna do, I talked to Grant before the event, and I asked him if he'd be okay if I made you guys a special offer, because I know that you guys are gonna need the software, it needs more training, there's more stuff I can give you, he said that he was totally okay with me making you guys a special offer, if you were okay with me, you guys okay if I spent about five or 10 minutes to make you guys a really special offer and make this whole process really, really simple for you, you guys okay with that? I ask for permission and I wait. What happens is always people say, yes, yes, please, take my credit card, it's really cool. I think once I was in a smaller group and I did that and nobody said anything, I was awkward, and I did this really weird thing that worked really good, so I'm gonna give you that, because it works, so I said it, I was like, so I did it and then I was, and nobody said anything, I was like, okay, well, because I already know this, I can go back to the room if you guys are good, because I already know this, I'm okay with it, I started kinda walking already, and people are like, no, no, no, tell us, tell us, are you sure? They're like, yeah, please tell us, I'm like, all right, I'll come back. So I ask for their permission as I transition, like that's it, so the way I do it is, let me ask you guys a quick question, are you guys okay if I spend five or 10 minutes really quick making a special offer, just gonna make all the stuff we talked about really, really easy to implement in your life, and they're like, yes, cool, now they give you permission, then you can go for as long as you want, nobody will walk out, nobody will be upset, nobody will be expecting you to sell, and they're really, really cool with it, okay? So that's the key, all right? Now this is where we come back to the stack slide. So the transition, then we got the stack. So here's the stack slide we ended with in the first presentation, right? We're gonna get this, you're gonna get this, you're gonna get this, here's the thing that we're gonna get, total value, okay? Now a couple of things I wanna bring back to this now, so what's interesting is we went through here, I would talk about their fault, we saw the vehicle, about their internal and their external. So when I'm creating my offer here, these are actually syncing up together, okay? So I make this offer, like this is typically, the first component of my offer typically is like me teaching the process that gets them into this vehicle, right? If it's, even as a supplement, like if I'm selling a supplement, the first thing I'm teaching is a training course and how I actually maximize the supplement to be able to get the result that they want, right? So it's like a training portion. And then when I'm creating the next part of the offer, it's not so much I'm creating more training, I'm looking at each of these things here, and like what do I have I can give them that's gonna make it so this external fear is no longer here, right? So for this one it's traffic, like I don't know about traffic, so I'm gonna make part of my offer here, something that addresses this external fear. Some people still have that fear or it might reemerge, whatever, so I wanna knock that down in the offer as well. Some people, it's internal, so I have part of my offer that relates to the internal, part of people that are coming back here, right? If you go to my offer that I'm creating for people, as you'll see when we go through the whole presentation, the parts of the offer are talking about the vehicle, right? So the vehicle, I got the training and the software that gives you the vehicle, right? Internal fear, like I don't know how to write copies, so I got funnel scripts in here, external, I'm gonna get traffic, okay, so we got traffic secrets in here, so like when you're creating the offer, it's matching what you're doing here, that's why they kind of go together, if that makes sense. A couple other things, notice like, it doesn't always have to be more training. A lot of times people are like, six week course on this, and on this, and on this, and on this, and it's like too overwhelming for people. Some of the best bonuses, one of the best bonus I ever did is we teach people a high ticket system for selling really expensive things on the phone, and I have a two page script that's done, for me, over $10 million in sales, that I can track back over a billion dollars in sales from people I know that use it, and so when I sell that program, one of my bonuses is a two page script, and that pushes Neil more than like a six week training course would, right? So it's like, I'm always looking like, when I'm doing this process, what are all the byproducts that happen during the process, right? What are the other things that are created? Like, if I was selling, if I was selling like a business in a box, I might be like, here's the legal documents you need to create your business in your hometown, here's the, the place I get my logos from, like all the things that they are gonna need, they're gonna keep them back, right? Again, come back to what we talked about in the earlier session, if you're selling the phone, right? What are all the different things you can include in here to make this the most irresistible offer, make sure they're gonna be successful what you're creating, okay? So the stack slide is kinda key of this. Now, it's concept of the stack, I learned from a guy named Armand Moran, and it was back when I was first starting this whole public speaking thing, and I was selling on stage, I was doing okay, but nothing like big, and I got invited to speak at Armand's big seminar, which for me was exciting, the first seminar I ever went to was Armand's, this was like three or four years later, he invited me to speak at, I was like so excited, and two weeks before I went to speak at, I was at another event, it was Dan Kennedy's big event, and Armand was one of the speakers there, and so I'm sitting in the audience watching everything, and I see Armand get up there on stage, and he does this thing, and he by far outsold everybody else, like it wasn't even close, and he was like the last, one of the last speakers of the last day, so I watched him do his whole pitch, his clothes, he sold everybody, and then I kinda lingered while everyone's leaving, and the end of it was just like a couple people in the ballroom left, and he came up to me, he had a stack of order forms, it was like this fat, and he walked up, he said, you know what these are? I'm like, a stack of order forms, he's like, nope, each of these is the $2,000 bill, and I was like, holy cow, I was like, how much is it? And he was like, I don't remember, it was like $400, or $500, and I was just like, what in the world? And he's like, you wanna stack order forms like this in my event in two weeks? I was like, yeah? He's like, okay, I'm gonna teach you one thing, and if you do this, you'll end up with a stack of order forms, if you don't do it, you won't. I was like, okay, what is it? He's like, I did it during the presentation, did you see it? I'm like, I don't know. I saw the presentation, but I don't really know what you did. He's like, it's this concept that we call the stack and the close, we call it the stack. And I said, well, how does it work? He said, okay, I'm gonna explain it to you, and then I'll send you my slides, you can see it, but this is the concept. He said, what most speakers do, is they do all their stuff up front, and they create an offer, and what they do in the offer is they're like, the first thing you're gonna get is this, they give them like the best thing up front, and they're like, the next thing you're gonna get is this and they give them the next best thing, the next thing you're gonna get is this, and this, and this, and this, and he said, the human mind can only remember one thing at a time, so what happens is, they have the best thing, and they talk about their next-best thing, and their third-best, fourth-best, fifth-best, and they get the last-best thing, and they have the last-best thing here, and he's like, and then they go and they talk about the price, and he said then they're talking about the price right here. He says if you look at that, he's like, all your mind remembers is the last thing, So the worst bonus then they associate with the price is that they look at that and they're like that's not worth $2,000 or $3,000 Whatever it is. He's like they try to do the whole that all the rest of their their closing stuff like that He says that they do that He's what happens is that the cells that they take he said he said I developed this process called the stacks He said what I do is I take my stack slice and here's the first thing you're gonna get and he talks about it And they get all excited like it says what that means you're gonna get this He said I have stacks like this first thing you're gonna get and then there's the price down below the total value Then I'm going to component number two of the bonus I talk about that and all the numbers that and they come back to the stacks like it's okay So what that means you're gonna get number one and number two like oh my gosh I get both those total value of this and he goes back into is like here's the third component This is this they comes back to it So that means you get one two and three so every single time come back to the stack side back to the stack side And the very last thing you show them is the stack side with everything on there And then you reveal the price now they're associated the price with everything as opposed to just bonus number 12, right? And also it's like oh, that's definitely worth it. He's like if you do the stack He's like you'll make way more money and I was like, oh my gosh This is scary and nervous, but I was like I trust him so I put it together two weeks later I went to his event did my stack and it was the first time I ever made over a hundred thousand dollars I think it did like hundred and my take home was 80 so hundred sixty thousand dollars in sales Doing it and I remember at first I was funny because I felt kind of like weird and awkward doing it Because the first time you do it's like this is kind of weird But I made 180,000 or excuse me hundred and sixty thousand I walked over 80,000 from and I was just like I can't be 80,000 dollars in a day and I started I did the stack one or two more times But then I felt kind of weird doing it so like I'm not gonna do it anymore So now I went to the next presentation I pulled the stack out did my whole presentation and was like crickets I was like that's weird so another presentation saving crickets and I was like what the crap I was like the only thing difference is the stacks to put the stack back in next time I spoke over a hundred thousand dollars again sales I was like I will never do presentation in the history of my life ever again Why don't do the stack and I haven't and it has made me literally hundreds of millions of dollars I'm not saying like so my hundreds million like click phones are over a hundred million dollars is all based on this Really good software with a stack and so the stack is the key like everything type of offer earlier It's the key and stack slide how we do the presentation is is important So when you guys watch this with me together, I want you to watch we come in here I'm gonna do the my origin story secret one secret two secret three I'm gonna break all the false beliefs master of mission to sell to you and then come through I'm gonna do the stack slide Okay, I'm gonna offer number one talk about it boom off to offer three out before you see me do the whole stack slide Okay, the end of it then we come back and we have like here's the total value So for me the number was eleven thousand five hundred fifty two dollars and my tell me said does that number look familiar for anybody here? I was like remember earlier say I told you guys that what if I can convince you that funnels are the key to tension your business She had to pay me eleven thousand five hundred fifty dollars and all of you is committed to that so right now Basically, that's the price so how many guys are you know, I like I asked him about it's like that's that's the price we talked about earlier and You guys all said yes to that so again the price marinated happened came back And this was the total value was the actual price and so that what's cool about this is I'm not gonna charge you guys that but I want to ask you a question. I learned this from Dave van Hoos. He calls these if all statements as I need them to believe that this is actually worth that and it is but I they need they need to believe it as well I believe it is they need to believe it as well. So again, I get them do it here They have a price marinated the whole time I come here. I get to eleven thousand five hundred fifty two dollars They see it now. I need them to say that it is worth that at least three times So Sam not gonna charge you eleven thousand five hundred fifty two dollars But let me ask you a question if all that this did was give you blank tie back the vehicle If all this did was give you a funnel we put a dollar advertising ending at two dollars back out Would it be worth eleven thousand five hundred fifty two dollars? And everyone that says yes And so okay, let me ask another question if all of this did Like the internal let's give you the ability to get your message out to more people would it be worth it? Yes That's the question if all that this did was give you the ability to finally stop having glue together 30 different systems to Build a funnel would be worth eleven thousand five hundred fifty two dollars Yes Okay, so for an hour they convinced themselves to work eleven thousand five hundred three times in a really told me yes Yes, yes, we're eleven thousand five hundred dollars now I'm gonna drop the price now. I can I drop the price down to what's actually gonna be now when I say it's only three thousand dollars They're like dude. I'm in Table Rush people sprinting to all different angles if you saw that Okay, so that's kind of that's kind of the the framework now You know those other things I'm gonna talk about there's different ways. I do my clothes is throughout here. There's different stuff There's things that I do on a webinar that I can't do a live event stuff like that So I'm talking about a couple of those things you see as are aware it takes notes on stuff. I want to talk about Just Okay, just so you can just see some of the nuances you understand they're different from this versus a webinar So if I was on a webinar when I do my webinar, it's actually a two-hour process. Okay, so here's here's the clocks, right? The first hour is this right here The second 30 minutes is the stack of the clothes here and if I'm on a webinar I spend another 30 minutes doing Q and not Q they are Q&As but like me just closing over and over and over again I'm not reading QA as people send me because sometimes you get hecklers that like throw you off But I I have pre-written like a 30 minutes of Q&As I just Q I keep doing Q&As call I answering questions and pushing people to buy right this people have like one concerns like knowing them So I just like try to answer every concern can dream of to get them to buy and so for a webinar It's a two-hour process when I'm in an event. I don't have the I don't have the time to do Q&A So I it's only 90 minutes. I don't have the Q&A. Okay, so there's cool things you can do in the Q&A that you can't do at a live webinar Or live event And if you could even if someone will give you Q&A time don't take it You don't want it'll kill the momentum at a live event if people are asking questions You'll kill everything okay events are all about momentum I have to get people stepping up and moving towards the back if you if people see other people moving there's a social proof Starts happening. It's really really cool. Okay, so you'll notice like when I spoke with the first 10x event I speak normally on stage as soon as I introduce the price I give people permission. I show slides explaining it And hopefully you'll see how we snuck attack the order forms in the pens I can't remember how the footage looks but that's kind of that was the concept there A couple of things that are interesting and it's harder to do like a tennis event because like this anyway If I'm in a traditional event There's a lot of stuff NLP and anchoring you can do in the room is kind of fun If you've ever watched Robin Williams his old stand-up comedy stuff He's the best this he would go and he would you work the stage or the low He'd like pick an anchor like a chair right and he would be telling a story He'd walk over every time you do the punchline He like he put his hand on the chair as he told the punchline story You do that three or four times in a row and it would happen after that And he'd walk over and if you needed to laugh He would just touch the chair and people were so anchored to the chair mean equals laughter subconsciously they would start laughing So like he would control it He would create that he would create that emotional connection to the chair and he'd use that throughout the presentation when he needed to laugh different times Or jokes where it's funny. He's still anchored to it and it would make people laugh after that When I'm speaking from stage or the same thing like if I know that my goal is to get people to the back of the room And I know that there's the sales table, but over here's the exit door We're not gonna buy when I'm talking about things I'm gonna anchor stuff. I'm like you're talking about success I'm gonna be pointing to the back of the room where the tables are at every time I talk about failure I'm gonna point to the exit door right so I might do I'll be successful life You gotta be focused you gotta be getting up you gotta be moving things and I'm always like pushing people Subconsciously to where I need them to go to be successful and they're talking about failure. I'm talking about the exit door I'm pointing to the exit door consistently as I'm talking about things like that Okay, so I'm put I'm anchoring people and pushing them to the things like that subconsciously and it's weird little things That don't seem like they matter until you do a lot of speaking presentations weekend after weekend You're testing stuff and you're like the audience is the same everything's the same except for I added this little piece into it Another thing I want you guys notice sorry These are just things I'm trying to remember top of my head that are that are important as we're queuing up for years to watch it Ted Thomas I talked to you about before who's the Pied Piper Who'd get people to like follow him with the credit cards in hand? It was awesome and Then the last thing I'll talk about I don't know if we had the video of this might have it I don't think I executed it perfectly, but but it still worked to push another 250,000 dollars in sales through When we did 10x event and then the fun hockey live event did another by two and a half million dollars in sales It's a thing you call the repitch and I learned this from Barry and blue who do the who help run our events And so happens that 10x event we did this whole thing right and what happens when people buy from this process It's just things like an emotional purchase, right? Like they go through the stories and the false beliefs are breaking and like they have this emotion and they whole things Choreograph to emotionally connect somebody and get them to do what you need them to do so you can get them the result that they want, right? But there's always like the logical people like well logically I don't know if this makes sense and like they're sitting back there and doing all sorts of stuff So the repitch is where you come back later and try to logically close people. Okay now after this happened We weren't planning on doing a repitch So I nothing planned other than like that night The night after maybe the morning at you know Grant and Jared like hey Can we come back on stage like ten more minutes to do a real quick repitch? I think we can close more sales and they're like whatever you did three million dollars yesterday You can do every one so came back and I just did a repitch and my repitch again I'm not super proud of it. It didn't extra $50,000 and so I think I could a lot more if I would have like structured better But the goal of the repitch is to logically convince people okay? It's the people who like emotionally weren't sold all along they're sitting there in bed They're like nervous awkward like mad they didn't do it. Okay my next day And I give them a logical proof of why they need to buy and now it's like okay I'm in and now the logical buyers buy the next day because they've had this an emotional weird thing that's driving crazy They don't understand it. They just know it's not logical and I've come back with logical proof I logically need to understand that that you know that sells phones are the key We're the only logical option makes sense like and I'll say the word logically five or six times Dean grassy I'm about this to you. And we just get started during this break We're also going to let you guys come access to this training course as well and that push another 250 thousand dollars in sales People who logically need that extra boost to kind of push them over okay, so I was the repitch get fun like you live We did the same thing the next day We did a repitch that did another two and a half million dollars in sales And so it's it's a big key of like getting people if you're able to do that on webinars now We're kind of modeling that as well where you do the webinar the first day and then the last day before cart closes coming back with a very emotional Sales video to pitch them on the emotional or the shimmy the logical side of it I'd push the last group people over the the edge, okay? and I think that that Those are the core things I want to talk about obviously there's so many more nuances about this whole thing We can spend I'm literally I've been three-day events teaching this stuff the books all about like like how do you craft the headline to do this?
